title: Sorry, Pastels — This Wedding Guest Dress Color Always Wins
text:
Sorry, Pastels — This Wedding Guest Dress Color Always Wins
It’s officially wedding season, and weekends are filling up fast with invites, travel plans, and outfit decisions. While this stirs up feelings of excitement and anticipation, it inevitably leads to the age-old (slightly panicked) question: “What am I going to wear?!” Not only do you want to look your best for a highly documented occasion, but you want to stay appropriate for the moment.

More importantly, unspoken rules matter. Steering clear of anything too close to white, cream, ivory, and even butter yellow is in your best interest. The key is looking polished without distracting from the bride—which narrows down the field more than you’d think. Enter the color red.

Red is a standout fashion choice that is wedding-approved when styled right. It reads confident, celebratory, and intentional, photographing beautifully and working across seasons. And it’s not just a sultry option, either; red can feel soft, romantic, and even playful depending on the silhouette and fabric.

This versatility is exactly what makes it work across a range of dress codes. Whether you’re heading to a black-tie affair or a whimsical garden party, the fiery hue transcends it all. A silky slip dress commands elegance, while a billowy midi gown echoes romance. Accessories can then dial the look up or down, shifting the mood to match the occasion. 

Red is having a major moment this wedding season, making a strong case for the cherry hue. "If you’re ready to try it for yourself, then keep reading to discover the 17 red wedding guest dresses fit for every style and setting.

title: The Sweet-Meets-Savory Fragrances That Smell Unlike Anything Else
text:
The Sweet-Meets-Savory Fragrances That Smell Unlike Anything Else Two decades ago, the mere mention of a gourmand perfume — with edible, dessert-like notes — was enough to conjure memories of Victoria's Secret body spray and the candy-striped interiors of your local store (a time when a tutti-frutti body mist was the marker of peak social status).

Thankfully, for any Zilennials like me, our olfactory tastes have grown up with us. Fast forward to the present, and the modern gourmand has become the blueprint for evoking scent memory and nostalgia, albeit all grown up, characterized by approachable yet complex profiles.

In a world that often feels chaotic, the popularity of sweeter, gourmand scents speaks to our collective craving for a little indulgence and a lot of familiarity. But if you’re a fragrance fence-sitter like me, the latest wave of gourmand releases pairs decadent sweet notes with unexpected savory elements, making these scents more intriguing and distinctive than ever before.

From velvety butternut to tomato-infused sprays, we’ve rounded up our favorite food-inspired fragrances for your perusal.

At Refinery29, we’re here to help you navigate this overwhelming world of stuff. All of our market picks are independently selected and curated by the editorial team. title: I Turned My Apartment Into The Swankiest Boutique Hotel With These 9 Essentials
text:
I Turned My Apartment Into The Swankiest Boutique Hotel With These 9 Essentials If you've ever moved, you know it's somewhere in the top five worst adult experiences. Between packing boxes, organizing furniture, changing addresses, and discovering things you haven't touched in years, it's a lot. Thankfully, Roadway Moving made the process significantly easier by handling all the heavy lifting, packing, and sorting out my boxes. Once everything was unpacked, I could finally focus on the fun part: designing my home.

As someone who spends a good chunk of the year staying in hotels for work, I've become a bit of a hotel snob. I've stayed everywhere from luxury resorts in Portugal to boutique properties in Singapore, and every trip I come home with new inspiration.

When it came time to decorate my new apartment, I knew exactly what I wanted: a home that felt like a boutique hotel you never wanted to check out of. Warm textures, calming colors, plush bedding, layered lighting, and pieces that feel elevated without feeling untouchable.

Here are the products that helped me bring that vision to life.

title: These Are The Most Popular OPI Nail Polish Shades Of All Time
text:
These Are The Most Popular OPI Nail Polish Shades Of All Time

OPI has been creating iconic nail polish colors for 45 years. In fact, a bottle from one of its most popular three shades (more on those later) is sold every 20 seconds around the world, with nearly two million bottles sold per year. Jill Bartoshevich, the brand’s global marketing director, tells Refinery29 that it’s a combination of salon-quality nail lacquer and quirky names that makes OPI so coveted. “We are known for our salon quality and are still in professional salons today,” she says. “Our bottle is patented and was made with salon pros in mind with our ergonomic design.” It’s little wonder, then, that these cult-favorite classics drive massive brand loyalty.

With that in mind, we asked Bartoshevich to share the most popular OPI shades of all time, so if you’re thinking about adding to your nail polish collection, or stuck for what to ask for at the salon, you’re in luck. From juicy red apples to snow white, there’s a color for everyone.

OPI Infinite Shine Big Apple Red, $14.49

Big Apple Red was created 26 years ago and still stands the test of time. This cool-toned classic red suits all skin tones, and the Infinite Shine variety delivers long-lasting wear with up to seven days of glossiness. The formula won’t damage your nails, and it’s one way to make a statement on fingers and toes this summer.

OPI Infinite Shine Bubble Bath, $14.49

Bubble Bath — adored by celebrities and royalty alike — was OPI’s first buildable sheer nude shade, easy to use and universally flattering. The non-streaky formula is chip-resistant and perfect for those who want something a bit closer to neutral.

OPI Infinite Shine Alpine Snow, $14.49

Alpine Snow by OPI is a fresh, crisp white that looks just as striking on fingernails as it does on toes. Two coats will ensure the shade remains clean and glossy for days on end.

OPI Infinite Shine Lincoln Park After Dark, $14.49

Bartoshevich says Lincoln Park After Dark started the dark nail revolution. The vampy purple is made for all seasons, and the punchy pigment means you can easily make a statement, even with one coat.

OPI Infinite Shine Black Onyx, $11.99

The first true black from OPI is definitely one to add to your collection of dark and brooding polishes. Whether you want to channel your inner emo kid or rock a neutral dark statement, this suits everybody.

OPI Nail Lacquer OPI’m a Bubble Bunny, $11.99

A new addition to the most popular OPI shades is OPI’m a Bubble Bunny. “[It] brings together two of the most-requested neutrals of all time — Bubble Bath and Funny Bunny — in a soft, milky hue with the slightest hint of pink,” says Bartoshevich. She adds, “This shade was born out of the viral trend of combining two coats of Funny Bunny and one coat of Bubble Bath, illustrating the full-circle effect of our brand inspiring a community, who in turn inspires the brand.”

title: Your Horoscope This Week: June 14 To 20
text:
Your Horoscope This Week: June 14 To 20

Cosmic beings, welcome to one of the freshest weeks of 2026. We’re still very much under the influence of the Gemini new moon that took place on June 14th, and because it occurred while both the sun and Uranus were in Gemini, there’s an undeniable spirit of reinvention in the air. If you’ve been wanting to rebrand, relocate, renovate, launch something, change your style, update your routines, or simply explore a different side of yourself, this lunation is giving you permission to experiment. You have until June 24th to actively work with these intentions, and because the Gemini full moon that completes this story won’t arrive until November 24th, the choices you make now have much longer-term implications than they may initially appear.

What’s interesting is that this new moon is ruled by Mercury, currently moving through Cancer and already approaching its retrograde shadow period. So while Gemini energy is encouraging curiosity, possibility, and movement, Mercury in Cancer is asking whether your decisions actually feel aligned. This week rewards calculated risks rather than impulsive ones. Explore the possibilities, but make sure your choices are connected to intuition rather than boredom, pressure, or fear of missing out. Gemini Season wants movement, but Mercury in Cancer reminds us that not every open door is meant to be walked through.

Then on June 19th, Chiron enters Taurus for a three-month preview of a much larger chapter that begins in April 2027. Chiron, an asteroid often called the Wounded Healer, represents the places where we carry insecurities but also develop wisdom through healing. Since 2018, Chiron in Aries has taught collective lessons around identity, courage, independence, and self-advocacy. Chiron in Taurus shifts the focus toward self-worth, stability, money, the body, pleasure, and our relationship with security itself. Unlike Uranus in Taurus, which often brought sudden external change, Chiron in Taurus works more quietly, helping us heal the foundation beneath our lives. As the week comes to an end, stay open to the possibilities Gemini Season is revealing while remaining grounded in what truly supports you. Reinvention becomes much more sustainable when it’s built on a foundation that can actually hold it.

Read your horoscopes for your Sun and Rising signs for the most in-depth forecast.

DashDividers_1_500x100

Aries Sun & Rising:

Aries, there’s something liberating about realizing you no longer need to keep everything inside. This week’s Gemini new moon working its magic through your communication, creativity, and connection sector, and many Aries will feel like a door has opened mentally. Conversations flow more easily, ideas arrive more rapidly, and a writing project, business idea, or new way of introducing yourself to the world may be asking for more space to breathe. The beauty of this new moon is that it rewards curiosity over perfection. Share, draft, post, pitch, brainstorm, and explore without needing to know exactly where it’s all leading. Whatever intentions you’re setting now around communication, creativity, learning, or connection have powerful growth potential between now and the Gemini full moon on November 24th.

Then on June 19th, a chapter that has defined much of your growth since 2018 begins to shift. Chiron, the asteroid associated with wounds and healing, leaves your sign and enters Taurus for a three-month preview. Chiron in Aries helped you become more resilient, self-aware, and authentic, even when growth felt uncomfortable. Now the focus moves toward money, security, and self-worth. The question is no longer who you are, but what helps you feel safe and supported. A beautiful way to honor this transition is to thank the version of yourself that carried you through the past eight years and then make one intentional investment in your future, whether that’s raising your rates, strengthening your savings, nourishing your body, or simply treating your needs as worthy of being prioritized. Chiron in Taurus will teach you that security is something you build from the inside out.

Taurus Sun & Rising:

Taurus, this Gemini new moon week’s asking you to expand your definition of abundance. The new moon is activating your money, self-worth, and security sector, but because Uranus is also in Gemini, the message isn’t simply “make more money.” It’s “be open to new ways of making money, sharing your gifts, and creating stability.” As a Fixed Earth sign, you naturally appreciate consistency, but this week rewards thoughtful experimentation. If you were to imagine yourself six months from now feeling freer, wealthier, more confident, and more supported, what choices would Future You be glad you started making today? Whether it’s launching an offer, learning a new skill, raising your rates, investing in yourself, or creating an additional stream of income, the seeds planted now have powerful growth potential between now and the Gemini full moon on November 24th.

Then on June 19th, you experience one of the most significant shifts of the year as Chiron enters your sign for a three-month preview. Chiron’s spent the past eight years in your healing and subconscious sector, helping you process old grief, fears, self-sabotaging patterns, and emotional baggage that often operated behind the scenes. Now the focus becomes much more personal. Chiron in Taurus shines a light directly on your identity, your relationship with your body, your self-worth, and the ways you define security. Some Tauruses may become more aware of insecurities they’ve learned to hide, while others may feel called to make changes that better reflect who they’re becoming. The key is not to view this as something being “wrong” with you. Chiron reveals where healing wants to happen. A beautiful way to work with this transit is through embodiment: nourishing your body, spending time in nature, strengthening your finances, and creating routines that reinforce the message that your worth is inherent, not something you have to earn. This is the beginning of a much deeper relationship with yourself.

Gemini Sun & Rising:

Gemini, when you change, your entire reality tends to change with you. This Gemini new moon week is especially personal because the new moon, the sun, and Uranus are all activating your sign, putting the spotlight directly on your identity, goals, appearance, confidence, and the way you’re introducing yourself to the world. You’re always evolving, but right now other people are noticing it, too. Opportunities may arrive more easily, people may respond differently to your energy, and your manifestation powers are stronger than usual because you’re aligned with momentum rather than resistance. Have fun with that. Since Mercury, your ruler, is in Cancer moving through your money and self-worth sector, don’t forget your value in the process. Set intentions that honor not only who you want to become, but what you genuinely believe you’re worthy of receiving.

Then on June 19th, Chiron enters Taurus and brings a very different energy into the conversation. While everyone else may be noticing your glow-up externally, Chiron quietly asks what’s happening behind the scenes. Over the next three months, this preview transit begins activating your healing, solitude, and subconscious sector, helping you notice patterns that are easier to ignore when life gets exciting. This doesn’t have to be dramatic. It may simply look like realizing you’re more tired than you thought, recognizing a fear that’s still influencing your decisions, or needing more alone time to process all the growth taking place. The invitation is not to choose between inner work and outer success… it’s to remember they support each other. The more honest you are about what needs healing beneath the surface, the easier it becomes to sustain everything you’re building above it.

Cancer Sun & Rising:

Cancer, can you feel it? Your solar season is loading. This is the last full week of Gemini Season, and the Gemini new moon’s activating your spirituality, healing, and solitude sector, making this less about external achievements and more about preparing your spirit for what’s next. Think of it as the final chapter before a new personal year begins. What habits, beliefs, fears, or emotional patterns are you ready to leave behind? What would a spiritual glow-up actually look like for you? Because Mercury, the ruler of this new moon, is currently moving through your sign, you’re especially tuned in to your intuition right now. Trust the nudges. The intentions you set now are helping create the emotional and spiritual foundation for the next six months.

Then on June 19th, Chiron enters Taurus and begins a three-month preview in your friendship, community, and future vision sector. This transit may bring healing opportunities through your social world, but healing isn’t always comfortable at first. Some Cancers may find themselves grieving a friendship that’s naturally drifting apart. Others may need to have an honest conversation they’ve been avoiding. And some may realize that a friendship’s becoming stronger precisely because both people are finally being honest about their needs. Chiron in Taurus asks you to stop measuring the health of a friendship by the absence of conflict. Real friendship can survive accountability, boundaries, difficult conversations, and moments of disappointment. Over the next few months, pay attention to who creates safety for authenticity rather than perfection. The strongest connections in your life will be the ones that allow everyone involved to be human.

Leo Sun & Rising:

Leo, there’s a difference between being surrounded by people and feeling connected to them, and this Gemini new moon week wants you to focus on the latter. The new moon’s activating your friendship, community, and future vision sector, making this an incredibly powerful time to think about where you’re headed and who you’re building alongside. New friendships, collaborations, networking opportunities, online communities, and long-term goals can all receive a boost over the next six months. Because Mercury, the ruler of this new moon, is moving through your spirituality sector, trust your intuition when it comes to people. Not every opportunity needs to be pursued, and not every connection needs to become a close friendship. The intentions you set now should focus less on popularity and more on alignment. The right people will help you become more of yourself, not less.

Then on June 19th, Chiron enters Taurus and begins a three-month preview in your career, visibility, and reputation sector. This can bring up sensitivities around success, recognition, leadership, or being seen. Some Leos may realize they’re carrying old wounds around criticism, authority figures, imposter syndrome, or feeling like they have to prove their worth through achievement. Others may become more aware of how much their career path’s been shaped by other people’s expectations rather than their own desires. The good news is that Chiron’s purpose isn’t to create wounds, but to help heal them. Over the next few months, pay attention to where you seek external validation and where you’re ready to define success on your own terms. The strongest foundation for your next chapter won’t be built on applause. It’ll be built on self-trust.

Virgo Sun & Rising:

Virgo, this Gemini new moon week is asking you to think bigger about what you’re building and who you’re becoming publicly. The new moon’s activating your career, visibility, and long-term ambition sector, making this one of the most important weeks of the year for setting professional intentions. Whether you’re looking to grow a business, step into leadership, change industries, launch a project, or simply receive more recognition for your work, the seeds planted now can grow significantly between now and the Gemini full moon on November 24th. Because Mercury, the ruler of this new moon and your ruling planet, is moving through your friendship and community sector, opportunities are more likely to arrive through conversations, introductions, and community than through isolation this time around.

Then on June 19th, Chiron enters fellow Earth sign Taurus and begins a three-month preview in your expansion, spirituality, and belief sector. This can bring healing around old stories you’ve carried about what’s possible for you. Some Virgos may become aware of limiting beliefs around success, travel, education, visibility, faith, or taking up more space in the world. Others may find themselves questioning philosophies or goals that once felt certain. The purpose isn’t to create confusion but to help you build a belief system that genuinely supports your growth. Over the next few months, notice where you’re underestimating yourself, playing smaller than necessary, or waiting until you feel perfectly prepared before taking action. Chiron in Taurus reminds you that confidence isn’t always what comes before the leap. Sometimes it develops because you were willing to take it.

Libra Sun & Rising:

Libra, this Gemini new moon week is reminding you that your life gets bigger the moment you stop assuming you’ve already seen all your options. The new moon is activating your expansion, travel, education, and big-picture sector, making this a powerful time to set intentions around what you want to learn, experience, teach, or explore between now and the Gemini full moon on November 24th. Some Libras may feel called toward travel, others toward a new certification, spiritual practice, business venture, or simply a different way of looking at life. Because Mercury, the ruler of this new moon, is moving through your career sector, pay attention to opportunities that help you expand professionally as well as personally.

Then on June 19th, Chiron enters Taurus and begins a three-month preview in your intimacy, vulnerability, and shared resources sector. This can bring healing around trust, emotional reciprocity, financial partnerships, and the places where you’ve struggled to fully let people in. Some Libras may become more aware of old wounds around abandonment, dependence, rejection, or feeling like they have to keep everything balanced and pleasant even when deeper needs aren’t being met. Others may notice patterns around money, debt, support, or receiving help. Chiron in Taurus isn’t asking you to become less independent. It’s asking you to examine whether you’ve confused self-sufficiency with emotional safety. Over the next few months, the greatest healing may come from allowing yourself to be supported, seen, and understood without feeling like you owe perfection in return.

Scorpio Sun & Rising:

Scorpio, some new moons ask you to start something. This week’s Gemini new moon asks you to trust something. The new moon is activating your sector of intimacy, wealth, soulmate connections, and transformation, making it one of the most potent lunations of the year for you. Because Gemini energy is involved, growth may arrive through unexpected conversations, surprising opportunities, new collaborations, multiple income streams, or unconventional approaches to love and partnership. The key is releasing the need to control exactly how the manifestation unfolds.

If you’ve been setting intentions around financial abundance, deeper intimacy, emotional healing, investments, business partnerships, or calling in healthier relationship dynamics, the next six months can bring remarkable momentum. Stay open to possibilities that don’t look exactly like the plan you originally envisioned. Sometimes abundance arrives through a side door you hadn’t considered, and sometimes love deepens through shared experiences, laughter, adventure, and friendship just as much as through emotional vulnerability.

Then on June 19th, Chiron enters Taurus and begins a three-month preview in your relationship sector. This is a significant shift because it asks you to become more honest about patterns you’ve been carrying into love, dating, friendship, and partnership. Some Scorpios may recognize places where they’ve been abandoning their own needs or accepting less than they deserve. Others may notice ways they’ve protected themselves so intensely that genuine intimacy has struggled to fully reach them. Chiron’s purpose isn’t to blame or shame… it’s to reveal what wants healing. Over the next few months, pay attention to recurring themes in your relationships. The connections that are meant to grow with you will benefit from honesty, accountability, stronger boundaries, and clearer communication. The ones that can’t withstand those things may be showing you exactly why they’ve reached their expiration date.

Sagittarius Sun & Rising:

Sag, if there’s one thing this week’s Gemini new moon doesn’t want you doing, it’s trying to figure everything out by yourself. The new moon’s activating your partnership sector, making relationships the area of life with the greatest growth potential between now and the Gemini full moon on November 24th. Whether you’re single, dating, married, building a business, or strengthening friendships, this is a powerful week to set intentions around the kinds of connections you want to cultivate. The key is staying open. Sagittarius energy can sometimes assume freedom and commitment are opposites, but this new moon is here to show you that the right relationships expand your world rather than limit it.

Then on June 19th, Chiron enters Taurus and begins a three-month preview in your health, wellness, and routines sector. This transit asks an important question: does your daily life actually support the future you’re trying to build? Some Archers may become more aware of burnout, inconsistent habits, stress patterns, or the tendency to focus on the big vision while neglecting the small systems that make it possible. Others may notice that their relationship with their body needs more care, patience, and consistency. Chiron in Taurus is asking for sustainability. Over the next few months, pay attention to the routines that help you feel grounded, nourished, and supported. The bigger your dreams become, the more important it is to create a foundation strong enough to hold them.

Capricorn Sun & Rising:

Capricorn, this Gemini new moon week is proof that the smallest changes often create the biggest results. The new moon is activating your health, wellness, work, and routines sector, making this one of the most practical and productive lunations of the year for you. While other signs may be focused on reinvention through travel, romance, or big life pivots, your growth comes through the systems you build day by day. The next six months can bring meaningful improvements in your health, workflow, schedule, business operations, or overall quality of life if you’re willing to make adjustments now. Because Mercury, the ruler of this new moon, is moving through your relationship sector, don’t be afraid to ask for support, delegate, collaborate, or communicate your needs more clearly. You don’t have to carry everything alone to prove you’re capable.

Then on June 19th, Chiron enters fellow Earth sign Taurus and begins a three-month preview in your creativity, romance, pleasure, and inner child sector. This can feel surprisingly vulnerable because Capricorn energy is often more comfortable producing, achieving, and planning than simply enjoying. Over the next few months, you may become more aware of wounds around self-expression, being seen creatively, receiving affection, allowing yourself pleasure, or believing that joy has to be earned before it can be experienced. Some Capricorns may rediscover hobbies they abandoned years ago. Others may realize they’ve been approaching love with the same seriousness they bring to work. Chiron in Taurus gently asks you to reconnect with the parts of yourself that create, play, laugh, flirt, and dream without needing a measurable outcome.

Aquarius Sun & Rising:

Aquarius, this Gemini new moon week’s a reminder that life’s meant to be lived, not just analyzed. The new moon is activating your creativity, romance, pleasure, and self-expression sector, making this one of the most joyful and heart-opening lunations of the year for you. The next six months can bring exciting developments in love, dating, creative projects, passion pursuits, entrepreneurship, children, or simply your willingness to let yourself be seen more authentically. Because Gemini energy is playful and experimental, this isn’t about having a five-year plan for your happiness. It’s about following what sparks your curiosity and excitement. And because Mercury, the ruler of this new moon, is moving through your wellness and routines sector, pay attention to the habits that either support or suppress your creativity. The more space you make for joy in your daily life, the easier it becomes to attract more of it.

Then on June 19th, Chiron enters Taurus and begins a three-month preview in your home, family, and emotional foundation sector. This is a significant shift because it asks you to examine what security means beneath the surface. Some Aquarians may find old family dynamics resurfacing for healing. Others may become more aware of childhood narratives, emotional patterns, or living situations that no longer reflect who they’re becoming. Chiron in Taurus is less interested in the image of stability and more interested in whether you actually feel safe, supported, and at home within yourself. Over the next few months, pay attention to what brings genuine emotional grounding. The stronger your foundation becomes, the easier it’ll be to build everything else you dream of on top of it.

Pisces Sun & Rising:

Pisces, this Gemini new moon week’s inviting you to build a life that feels as good on the inside as it looks from the outside. The new moon’s activating your home, family, and roots sector, making this a powerful time to set intentions around where and how you want to live, who you want to surround yourself with, and what helps you feel emotionally secure. Some Pisces may be thinking about moving, redecorating, spending more time with family, creating stronger boundaries at home, or simply cultivating a greater sense of peace in their everyday environment. Because Mercury, the ruler of this new moon, is moving through fellow water sign Cancer in your creativity, romance, and joy sector, don’t overcomplicate the process. Ask yourself a simple question: what would make life feel more nourishing, more beautiful, and more like home?

Then on June 19th, Chiron enters Taurus and begins a three-month preview in your communication, mindset, and self-expression sector. This can bring healing around your voice, your confidence in your ideas, and the stories you tell yourself about whether you’re being heard or understood. Some Pisces may become more aware of moments when they stay silent to keep the peace, minimize their needs, or assume others should intuitively know what they’re feeling. Others may feel called to write, teach, create content, speak publicly, or express themselves in ways that feel more direct than usual. Over the next few months, pay attention to where you’re editing yourself unnecessarily. Healing may come through discovering that your voice deserves just as much space as everyone else’s in the conversation.

title: Everything We Know About The Love Hypothesis Movie Starring Lili Reinhart
text:
Everything We Know About The Love Hypothesis Movie Starring Lili Reinhart

Piners, yearners, and lovers, rise up. After what has felt like a centuries-long romantic dry spell, it appears we’re finally entering a rom-com renaissance. A flurry of YA book-to-television adaptations is filling our screens — and our hearts — proving that studio execs may have finally cracked the code for what makes a swoon-worthy love story. The latest gem in this resurgence? The Love Hypothesis. Adapted from Ali Hazelwood’s best-selling novel, it follows Olive Smith — a third-year PhD candidate with a fiercely guarded heart — and young professor, Dr Carlsen, as they embark on a fake relationship that challenges both their theories and their scepticism in love.

From what began as fanfiction based on the Star Wars characters Rey and Kylo Ren, the final story was adapted into a modern tale, with rounds of casting taking place in July 2025.

Here’s everything we know about The Love Hypothesis so far.

When will The Love Hypothesis be released?

Prime Video has just confirmed The Love Hypothesis will be released on September 23, 2026. You will be able to watch it on Prime Video.

What is The Love Hypothesis about?

Dubbed by #BookTok as a STEM romance, the film centres around Olive Smith (Lili Reinhart), a third-year PhD candidate at Stanford University, who begins a fake relationship with brooding yet brilliant professor, Dr Adam Carlsen (Tom Bateman), in a bid to prove to a friend that she’s over her ex.

As their trope-filled arrangement unfolds, their fake feelings begin to develop into something real, and the pair are forced to face their vulnerabilities, feelings, and undeniable attraction, throwing everything they thought they knew about love into disarray.

Who will be in The Love Hypothesis movie?

In mid-2025, Deadline announced that beloved Riverdale alum Lili Reinhart would be stepping into the shoes of Olive Smith, while also serving as an executive producer on The Love Hypothesis. Since then, Reinhart has been dropping some behind-the-scenes teasers and first-look content on her TikTok, keeping fans on their toes. Joining her as the brooding Dr Adam Carlsen is Tom Bateman (Death on the Nile). The film will be directed by American filmmaker Claire Scanlon, with Ali Hazelwood herself lending her world-building expertise as an executive producer.

Is there a trailer for The Love Hypothesis?

While no official trailer has been released for the film adaptation of Ali Hazelwood’s The Love Hypothesis, Prime Video recently released a teaser alongside some first-look footage in a sizzle reel showcasing the streaming platform’s upcoming releases.

title: 10 Differences Between Every Year After And Carley Fortune’s Book
text:
10 Differences Between Every Year After And Carley Fortune’s Book

Every Year After is our current obsession, as the new yearning TV show on Prime Video ticks all the boxes. However, it does deviate from the source material a fair bit. Fans of Every Summer After by Carley Fortune may be surprised by some of the twists and turns, but Abigail Cowen, who plays Delilah Mason, believes they will draw people in. “We expand on other characters, and I think that’s really fun,” she tells Refinery29 Australia. “[With] Delilah, we get to see a lot more of her and what she’s going through. Same with… Chantal. She got to kind of have that creative liberty.” While the star admits some are a bit of a shock, fans will likely be hooked and want to see where the storylines go.

Below, we go through the biggest changes between Every Year After and Every Summer After by Carley Fortune.

Barry’s Bay is now on the West Coast

In the book, Barry’s Bay is set in Ontario, Canada, while Percy comes from Toronto. In Every Year After, the show switched it up. Barry’s Bay now sits in British Columbia, and Percy is from Seattle in the US.

Percy is now an obituary writer

Percy’s career in Every Year After is a little worse for wear than in the book. While she was a senior editor of a home decor magazine alongside Chantal, the TV show downgraded her to an obituary writer. It helps show how her regret over the past decade has affected her life.

Percy and Sam’s first kiss takes place during the anatomy scene

In the novel, Percy and Sam originally kiss in 2013 after watching The Blair Witch Project together. In Every Year After, the pair end up kissing in 2014 during the infamous anatomy scene.

Chantal and Jordie’s new romance

Chantal was a big addition to Every Year After, as in the novel, she doesn’t actually travel to Barry’s Bay. In Fortune’s book, Chantal appears when she speaks to Percy on the phone. This means her romance with Jordie is completely new and unexpected.

Delilah is now married and is having an affair with Charlie

In the novel, Delilah was mainly seen as Percy’s childhood friend. While she attended college with Sam, she wasn’t involved in the present-day timeline. In Every Year After, Delilah is shown to be in a rocky marriage and is busy having an affair with Charlie.

Delilah might be queer

During the last episode, there’s a glimpse of Delilah kissing a woman. It’s unclear where her storyline is going, but Cowen tells Refinery29 Australia she’s interested in seeing this queer journey for Delilah. “I think that would be a really cool path with Delilah. I think she’s in this place of just finding herself and taking care of herself for the first time,” the actor says.

Sue Florek’s storyline was expanded

The relationship between Sue and Percy is explored further in Every Year After, which adds a sweet side to the show. It shows how she acted as a second mother to Percy.

Percy inherits The Tavern after Sue’s death

Percy doesn’t inherit The Tavern in Every Summer After, but as a result of writers expanding Sue and Percy’s relationship, the TV show made a bold choice. This causes drama and tension between Percy, Charlie and Sam, and also gives Percy a reason to stick around in Barry’s Bay.

Sam breaks up with Percy via email before she sleeps with Charlie

In Every Summer After, Percy cheats on Sam with Charlie because Sam is distant while at college. In the TV show, Sam breaks up with Percy via email before she sleeps with Charlie. This makes Percy seem more forgivable, as no actual cheating took place.

Sam doesn’t find out about Percy and Charlie until he’s an adult

In the novel, Sam actually finds out about Charlie and Percy’s hook-up just months after it took place. In the TV show, Sam doesn’t know until he tries to restart his relationship with Percy in Episode 5. Percy finally decides to tell him the truth, which causes turmoil.

title: Matt Cornett & Sadie Soverall On The Art Of Longing In Every Year After
text:
Matt Cornett & Sadie Soverall On The Art Of Longing In Every Year After

There’s something undeniably irresistible about a great yearning romance. The stolen glances, the missed chances, the kind of connection that lingers long after two people part ways — it’s a formula that has captivated audiences for generations. Prime Video’s Every Year After, adapted from Carley Fortune’s bestselling novel, leans fully into that emotional territory, delivering a sweeping second chance love story that unfolds across years, memories, and the complicated realities of growing up in love with your best friend.

At the center of the series are best friends Sam and Percy, played by Matt Cornett and Sadie Soverall, who bring the story’s emotional core to life with performances that feel both intimate and expansive. Whether the series succeeds overall may be up for debate on our timelines and BookTok (I think you’ll enjoy the series if you liked the book!), but the two young actors at the center are great together. And together, they navigate a relationship shaped as much by timing and circumstance as it is by chemistry, capturing the bittersweet push and pull that makes second-chance romances so compelling. The result is a series that understands the appeal of yearning (the word of the year) and that a slow burn love story we can project all of our nostalgia and young adult angst onto is exactly what the summer needs.

For Cornett, best known from High School Musical: The Musical: The Series, Every Year After marks a chance to explore a more mature, emotionally layered character. Meanwhile, Soverall brings depth and vulnerability to a messy protagonist whose journey spans multiple stages of life — and mistakes.

Of course, bringing a beloved book to the screen comes with its own unique pressures. The BookTok community surrounding this source material came with strong attachments to the characters and a clear vision of how they should look, sound, and behave (and boy, do they have strong opinions about this one!). For Cornett and Soverall, that meant balancing fidelity to the novel with the freedom to make the roles their own. It also meant tackling some unexpected challenges along the way — including mastering accents (Soverall more than Cornett) and ensuring their performances remained consistent across different timelines and emotional stages.

As audiences continue to gravitate toward romance stories that prioritize emotional connection over spectacle, Every Year After arrives at a moment when yearning is having something of a cultural renaissance. Whether it’s in literature, television, or film, stories centered on anticipation, longing, and unresolved feelings are resonating in a major way. This series taps directly into that appetite.

Here, Refinery29 talks to Cornett and Soverall to discuss adapting a fan-favorite novel, the enduring appeal of yearning in romance, learning accents for their roles, and what they hope audiences take away from this emotional story.

Refinery29: There have been so many recent shows, even just on Prime Video, in this young adult romance genre, like Off Campus, The Summer I Turned Pretty, and Maxton Hall, just to name a few. What would you say sets this show apart?

Sadie Soverall: I think the scope of this show is really unique. I mean, we’re following these characters from 13 to their mid 20s, we’re seeing so much of their life, and it feels really nostalgic, looking into the past and then into the present for these characters. It’s a really beautiful, sweeping, yearning love story, and there’s mistakes made, and it’s not perfect.

Matt Cornett: I second all that. I think getting to see these characters throughout their life, you see them in their most formative stages of their teenage years, and young adulthood, and going into their mid to late 20s. There’s so many formative chapters that you see in their lives that I think sets it apart a little bit, and was so much fun to play for us. I’m so excited for people to see.

Sadie, you mentioned yearning, Matt. One thing about Sam is that he’s a yearner.

MC: Boy, is he a yearner!

He’s also this book boyfriend that fans of Every Summer After already held so dearly in their heads and hearts. Matt, talk about the pressure – if any – that comes with bringing to life that yearning and this guy so many people fell in love with on the page.

MC: I think inherently there is a pressure that comes along with like bringing this this character to life when there’s already such a big beautiful fan base around it, but I think truly like the writing helps so much with us, you know. For us, I think obviously Carly’s writing in the book was such a great base to kind of start from, and then you know Amy’s the way that Amy adapted it into the show, I think that the writing made the yearning very easily, they wrote it very with a lot of yearning eyes, and so there I think there were probably some moments I threw a couple extra yearning looks in there, but there was definitely a lot written in there, and I just hope that people feel like we captured, you know, the Sam and Percy that they saw in their heads, and I know we’re both really proud of it.

I mean, men need to yearn more in general.

MC: I’m telling ya. Yeah!

Let’s talk spoilers! Percy’s big mistake. Sure, it’s not great to sleep with your boyfriend’s brother, but I feel like everyone gives her a bit too hard of a time about it. Sadie, is she really the villain in this story? Defend your girl.

SS: I think what is so brilliant about this story that really reflects real life. There’s so many characters who make mistakes, and I don’t feel like there are villains in the story. I feel like it’s people who have made mistakes are also good people. I think both things can be true at once. If you see the story and you know the book, and you know what happens, you know why Percy makes that decision. She makes this choice, and it’s really in the moment, and it’s also a choice she makes when she’s young, and I think a lot of people can relate to making a choice when they’re young that they don’t quite think through. It comes from a place of wanting to be loved. I understand that the discourse and the debate, and I think that’s what’s so brilliant about the stories, it makes you talk about these things, and it makes you debate.

It’s also so important to have to see a young girl not being perfect and making mistakes and that’s okay, especially a sexual mistake.

SS: Absolutely!

I want to talk about accents, because Matt, you are an American playing a Canadian, and Sadie, you are British playing an American. How much conversation and time and prep went into making sure those accents were accurate and true to your characters?

SS: I absolutely did my research. I love doing an American accent, and I feel like Percy’s one felt like really organic to me, because I’ve grown up watching American shows and American media, so it kind of just came quite instantaneously, but I did have to do my warm-ups every morning which you heard.

MC: I would hear them every single morning. [laughs] It was great.

SS: [laughs] We got into a good flow of it.

Matt, as a Canadian, I feel like the differences are so subtle, depending on where you are from. Were there any words that you out some Canadian into?

MC: I talked with [the showrunner] Amy a little bit about this, and me and Michael [Bradway who plays Charlie] talked a little bit about this. She was like, “I don’t really feel like I want you to put on a Canadian accent,” which, honestly, I don’t know if anyone would want me to [laughs]. You don’t want to hear me try to do an accent. I think that there were moments that I wanted to try and throw a little Canadianisms in there, and I wanted to, you know, say the ‘sorry’ and the ‘aboots’ and all that, but I think any moment that I would do that, our director would come up to me and be like, “Hey, maybe, maybe don’t do that.” [laughs]

SS: It organically filters in, because we were around the Canadian crew.

MC: Yeah! And also it was important, obviously, the book takes place in Canada, and we wanted to make sure the show took place in Canada and we wanted to pay homage to the Canadian roots of this story. And so I think that the show, in and of itself, really shows off Canada so beautifully, and it just paints such a beautiful picture of what Canada is. I love Canada, so I think that was enough. Luckily me and Michael didn’t really have to think about the accents of it all.

I was listening for a Canadian sowrry.

MC: I tried, I tried, but they were like, “maybe dial it back.” [laughs]

Every Year After is streaming now on Prime Video.
